sourceNote: Static MapNews seed data compiled from a top-news summary. Replace with an automated collector when the news ingestion pipeline is ready.

items.title: Trump and Xi expected to meet as pressure builds over Iran war
items.dek: Diplomatic focus shifts to Beijing as global leaders look for a path to contain the widening conflict.
content: The lead item in the global news cycle is a planned Trump-Xi meeting in China, framed around the accelerating Iran war and the search for diplomatic leverage.

The location matters because Beijing is positioned as both a diplomatic stage and a strategic actor. Any public signal from the meeting could affect energy markets, defense posture, and allied coordination.
MapNews marks Beijing as the story origin so readers can understand where the key diplomatic action is expected to unfold.

items.title: Kuwait alleges Iranian attack on Bubiyan Island
items.dek: The report raises regional security concerns around a strategically sensitive Gulf location.
content: Kuwait's allegation that Iran struck Bubiyan Island puts a Gulf flashpoint on the map and adds pressure to an already unstable regional picture.
Bubiyan Island sits near sensitive maritime routes, making the story important for security analysts, energy watchers, and governments tracking escalation risk.
The detail page keeps the focus on the geography of the incident and why a small island can become a large geopolitical signal.

items.title: Cruise passengers return to quarantine after fast-spreading illness
items.dek: Thousands of passengers are routed through Omaha as health officials monitor a shipboard outbreak.
content: A shipboard illness story becomes a land-based public health story as passengers return through Omaha for quarantine procedures.
The map marker highlights the operational endpoint where health monitoring, travel logistics, and public communication converge.
The event is less about one route and more about how quickly travel-linked outbreaks can become multi-agency response problems.

items.title: US consumer prices jump as energy costs surge
items.dek: War-driven oil price pressure shows up in Washington's latest inflation debate.
content: The inflation story is tied to conflict-driven oil prices, with Washington becoming the policy center for the consumer price shock.
A rise in energy costs can feed transportation, food, manufacturing, and household budgets, which makes the headline broadly relevant beyond financial markets.
MapNews places the story at Washington because the next phase is likely to be shaped by official data, policy reactions, and political pressure.

items.title: Wall Street slows as AI stocks pause and oil prices climb
items.dek: Market attention splits between cooling AI momentum and conflict-linked energy volatility.
content: Wall Street's latest move combines two forces that have shaped recent market behavior: AI enthusiasm and oil price risk.
A slowdown in major AI-linked names can change risk appetite, while higher oil prices can alter inflation expectations and sector rotation.
The New York marker anchors the story at the market venue where those global signals are converted into pricing decisions.

items.title: Starmer faces pressure after cabinet resignations
items.dek: Political turbulence in London grows as ministers step down and questions mount.
content: London is the center of a developing political story as Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces questions following ministerial resignations.
The event matters because personnel exits can become signals of policy conflict, internal party pressure, or public confidence problems.
The map view helps separate the Westminster political story from broader international headlines competing for attention.

items.title: Hegseth heads to Congress under renewed scrutiny
items.dek: Lawmakers prepare questions over military and war planning as oversight pressure increases.
content: Congressional questioning turns Washington into the focal point for oversight of military planning and conflict management.
The story is important because defense testimony can reveal policy constraints, operational assumptions, and partisan pressure points.
MapNews groups it separately from the inflation story even though both occur in Washington, because the underlying topic and reader intent are different.

items.title: Ukrainian presidential adviser faces money-laundering investigation
items.dek: Kyiv watches a high-profile probe involving a close adviser to President Zelenskyy.
content: A corruption-related investigation involving a senior Ukrainian presidential adviser puts Kyiv back into political focus.
The story matters because high-level investigations can affect wartime governance, reform credibility, and international confidence.
The map marker keeps the story grounded in Kyiv, where domestic political accountability intersects with global scrutiny.
